front: What is the role of efflux transporters in blood-brain barrier penetration?
back: P-glycoprotein (P-gp/ABCB1) and BCRP (ABCG2) actively efflux drugs from brain endothelial cells. CNS drugs must overcome efflux (low P-gp substrate affinity) or be designed as poor substrates. Co-administration of P-gp inhibitors can increase brain exposure.
